intro:
Once offered a refreshing concept: one hand-curated match per day, eliminating swipe fatigue. It attracted users exhausted by Tinder's infinite volume — but shut down in 2022.

If you were a Once user looking for a 2026 alternative with the same quality-over-quantity philosophy, Embir is the answer: intentional matching, verified profiles, full LGBTQ+ inclusivity, and free access.

comparisonTable.embir: Quality — with enough volume to match quickly

sections.title: Once's philosophy — reimagined for 2026
sections.content: Once understood what big apps ignore: infinite swipe is exhausting and counterproductive. One well-chosen match beats 100 random swipes. Embir applies the same philosophy with bidirectional filtering: you see fewer profiles than Tinder, but each one truly matches your criteria AND you're a potential match for them.
highlights: Fewer profiles = less noise = more real connections

verdict: Once closed in 2022. If you're looking for the 2026 alternative that honors the same quality-over-quantity philosophy — Embir is the answer, with verified profiles, LGBTQ+ inclusivity, and free access at launch.
ctaText: Once's philosophy, modern and free — join Embir

faq.q: Is there a Once alternative in 2026?
faq.a: Once closed in 2022. Embir shares the same intentional matching philosophy with bidirectional filtering and verified profiles.

faq.q: Does Embir apply curated selection like Once?
faq.a: Embir uses a compatibility algorithm rather than manual curation, but the spirit is the same: quality over quantity.
